title: Add Lessons Tab with Video List to Help Popout
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want a Lessons tab in the help window that lists all available tutorial videos, so that I can quickly find and watch training content without leaving the app.
description:
Add a "Lessons" tab to the help popout window that dynamically fetches and displays tutorial videos from the WordPress JSON API.

## Implementation
1. **Data source**: Fetch lessons dynamically from the WordPress REST API at `/wp-json/wp/v2/lesson/`. Thumbnails fetched via `wp:attachment` media endpoint using the lesson ID.
2. **Lesson list**: Display all available lessons with thumbnails in order
3. **Video playback**: Clicking a lesson opens a YouTube modal overlay so users can watch without leaving the page
4. **External link**: Close button and "New Window" button load the lesson page in a new browser tab (lesson URL from the JSON shortcode field)

## Data Fetching
- Lessons fetched dynamically from WordPress JSON API (server-side cached)
- Initially prototyped with hardcoded data, then switched to dynamic fetching
- Thumbnails retrieved via the `wp:attachment` relationship in the JSON response
acceptance_criteria: Help popout has a Lessons tab alongside existing tabs
Lessons tab shows all available lessons with thumbnails from the WordPress API
Clicking a lesson opens a YouTube video modal
Close button and New Window button open the lesson URL in a new browser tab
Lesson data is fetched dynamically from the WordPress REST API
Thumbnails are fetched dynamically from the wp:attachment endpoint
ux_changes: New Lessons tab in the help popout showing a list of tutorial videos with thumbnails. Clicking opens a YouTube modal. Close/New Window buttons navigate to the lesson page.
middleware_changes: Client-side JavaScript to fetch lesson data from WordPress REST API and render the list dynamically.
api_changes: Consumes WordPress REST API endpoints: /wp-json/wp/v2/lesson/ for lesson data and /wp-json/wp/v2/media?parent={id} for thumbnails.
implementation_notes: Initially prototyped with 3 hardcoded videos. Product owner directed dynamic fetching from WordPress JSON API. Thumbnails sourced from wp:attachment relationship. Estimated 4 hours (Medium). Lesson URL slugs needed validation to ensure correct redirects.
comment_summary: Product owner provided the WordPress JSON API endpoint for lessons. Developer initially hardcoded 3 videos, then switched to dynamic fetching. Thumbnails fetched via wp:attachment media endpoint. URL slug issues identified for one lesson. Estimated 4 hours implementation.
